zh.lifeSummary: 當Matilda de Warenne於1166年出生在英國素可列維斯時，她的父親Hamelin de Warenne Earl of Surrey37歲，母親Isabel de Warenne 4th Countess of Surrey30歲。她去世於1228年12月13日之前在法國諾門迪奧內多福洛特，埋葬在法國諾門迪瑟-馬利福卡爾蒙特。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Matilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mathilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Matilda de Warenne was born in 1166, in Lewes, Sussex, England, her father, Hamelin de Warenne Earl of Surrey, was 37 and her mother, Isabel de Warenne 4th Countess of Surrey, was 30. She died before 13 December 1228, in Domfront, Orne, Normandy, France, and was buried in Foucarmont, Seine-Maritime, Normandy, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Matilda de Warenne est née en 1166, à Lewes, Sussex, Angleterre, son père, Hamelin de Warenne Earl of Surrey, avait 37 ans et sa mère, Isabel de Warenne 4th Countess of Surrey, avait 30 ans. Elle est décédée avant 13 décembre 1228, à Domfront, Orne, Normandie, France, et a été enterrée à Foucarmont, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, France.

Sie starb vor 13. Dezember 1228 in Domfront, Orne, Normandie, Frankreich und wurde in Foucarmont, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Frankreich begraben.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Matilda de Warenne родилась в 1166 г. в местности львесь, суссекс, Англия, её отцу по имени Hamelin de Warenne Earl of Surrey было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabel de Warenne 4th Countess of Surrey, 30. Она умерла перед 13 декабрь 1228 г. в местности домфронт, орн, норманди, Франция и была похоронена в местности фоукармонт, сине-маритим, норманди, Франция.
